title: List all users
description: Return all columns for all rows in the users table.
explanation: Selecting all columns and rows from users returns the full table.

title: Orders per user
description: Return each user_id and the number of orders they have made as order_count. Include users with zero orders. Order by user_id ascending.
expected_query:
SELECT u.user_id, COUNT(o.order_id) AS order_count
FROM users u
LEFT JOIN orders o ON u.user_id = o.user_id
GROUP BY u.user_id
ORDER BY u.user_id
solution_query:
SELECT u.user_id, COUNT(o.order_id) AS order_count
FROM users u
LEFT JOIN orders o ON u.user_id = o.user_id
GROUP BY u.user_id
ORDER BY u.user_id;
explanation: A LEFT JOIN keeps every user. COUNT(order_id) counts non-NULL matches, so users with no orders get 0.
hints: Use a LEFT JOIN so users with no orders are included.
COUNT(order_id) counts the number of orders per user.

title: Total completed order amount
description: Return the total amount of all completed orders as total_completed_amount.
expected_query:
SELECT SUM(amount) AS total_completed_amount
FROM orders
WHERE status = 'completed'
explanation: Filter to completed orders and sum their amounts.

title: Users who signed up in 2023
description: Return all users who signed up in the year 2023. Include all columns.
expected_query:
SELECT *
FROM users
WHERE signup_date >= DATE '2023-01-01'
  AND signup_date < DATE '2024-01-01'
solution_query:
SELECT *
FROM users
WHERE signup_date >= DATE '2023-01-01'
  AND signup_date < DATE '2024-01-01';
explanation: Use a half-open date range to capture all dates in 2023 without worrying about time components.
